settings.gg0OF3RfwaT_text: 5-step guide to achieve glass skin
settings.ggDWYk8HtfY_text: Must-have products for achieving glass skin
settings.ggEUWPVM_pu_text: Here are some of the most prominent ingredients in Korean skincare, found in various types of K-Beauty cosmetics, offering numerous benefits for your skin:
settings.ggGFOC5RVjM_text: The most important step of all: stick to your routine! When you start seeing results, don’t stop—keep giving your skin the care it needs to maintain its glow. Since glass skin is closely tied to overall health, maintaining a balanced diet, staying hydrated, and leading an active lifestyle are just as essential. After all, your skin reflects what’s happening inside your body!
settings.ggGJGclMkS2_text:
Three words define glass skin: smooth, radiant, and clear. Originating in South Korea, glass skin is known as yuri pibu, which translates to transparent and luminous skin—qualities that mirror the flawless appearance of glass. 

 

According to skincare experts, this look is achieved when your skin is at its healthiest. As Korean skincare products and routines have gained global popularity, interest in glass skin has skyrocketed, making it a major skincare goal for many. But how can you achieve it?
settings.ggIsF3PCffy_text: You’ve probably heard sunscreen is the best anti-aging product – but it’s more than that. It also helps prevent skin damage, maintains an even tone, and supports your skin’s natural regenerative processes. That’s why it’s one of the most essential products for glass skin. Choose one that offers broad UVA/UVB protection, apply every morning, and reapply as needed.
settings.ggJYkAdT5kO_text: If you're just getting started on your glass skin journey, here’s a simple list of essentials to help you build your new skincare routine:
settings.ggNH1agqHMg_text:
Hydration is key to achieving flawless glass skin—it’s all about keeping your skin plump, smooth, and healthy! Start with a toner to balance your skin’s pH and prep it for better absorption. Follow up with:

  • Essence for deep hydration and skin repair
  • Serum to address specific skin concerns
  • Moisturiser (gel or cream) to lock in moisture and enhance glow

Remember to hydrate your skin every morning and night for the best results.
settings.ggRA4cODjof_text:
You can also add other products to your Korean skincare routine like sheet masks and facial mists for an extra hydration boost, anti-aging creams to maintain firmness and elasticity, and facial massage tools to improve circulation and help with product absorption.

 

Always look for ingredients that match your skin type and pay close attention to how your skin reacts to new products – that’s how you’ll find the best options for your routine. It all depends on your skin’s unique needs!
settings.ggRtqxKFh4j_text: What is glass skin and why is it so popular?
settings.ggUy_F5lkoQ_text:
There are two types of exfoliation:

  • Chemical exfoliation, using acids like glycolic or lactic acid
  • Physical exfoliation, which manually removes dead skin cells with ingredients like sugar or salt

Experiment with different exfoliants to find what works best for you. Exfoliation should be done once or twice a week, preferably at night, to avoid sun sensitivity.
settings.gger8fubMsj_text: Must-have products for achieving glass skin
settings.ggnOUrQR6gJ_text:
1. Oil-based cleanser
  2. Water-based gel or foam cleanser
  3. Exfoliant
  4. Hydrating toner
  5. Essence
  6. Repairing serum
  7. Gel or cream moisturiser
  8. Sunscreen
settings.ggoxIGOxvsy_text: What is glass skin and why is it so popular?
settings.ggsuc8uMOvV_text:
If you follow skincare trends, you've likely heard of glass skin—a Korean skincare concept that has been making waves in Europe as well. But what exactly is glass skin? And what skincare routine should you follow to achieve it?

 

In this post, we’ll cover everything about glass skin, from its origins to a step-by-step guide to achieving it, along with product recommendations that will make all the difference
settings.ggwb67qAUA9_text: Start with an oil-based cleanser to remove makeup and excess sebum. Follow up with a gentle water-based cleanser to get rid of dirt and impurities. This double cleansing method can be done once a day or both morning and night. Fresh, clean skin is the perfect canvas for your glass skin routine!
